name: Data Quality Audit
description: Scan contacts for missing required fields
prompt:
Search all {objectType} and check for records missing these critical fields:
- Contacts: email, firstname, lastname, lifecyclestage, hubspot_owner_id
- Companies: name, domain, industry
- Deals: dealname, amount, closedate, pipeline, dealstage, hubspot_owner_id

For each field, report: total records, records with value, records missing value, fill rate %.
Flag any field with < 80% fill rate as needing attention.
Sort results by fill rate ascending (worst first).

name: Pipeline Health Audit
description: Analyze deal pipeline health and stuck deals
prompt:
Get all deals grouped by pipeline and stage. For each pipeline report:
- Total deals and total value per stage
- Average days in each stage
- Deals stuck > 30 days in any stage (list them)
- Deals with close date in the past but still in open stage
- Deals with amount = 0 or null

Provide a health score for each pipeline: Green (no issues), Yellow (minor issues), Red (critical issues).

name: Association Gaps Audit
description: Find missing key CRM associations
prompt:
Find all contacts that have a non-empty 'company' text property but no associated company record.
Find all deals with no associated contact.
Find all deals with no associated company.

Report counts and list the first 20 of each for review.

name: Property Usage Audit
description: Identify low-value and duplicate properties
prompt:
List all custom properties for {objectType}. For each:
- Name, label, type
- Fill rate (% of records that have a value)
- Last time any record had this property updated

Flag properties with < 5% fill rate as deletion candidates.
Flag properties with > 50% fill rate that are not used in any workflow or list as under-utilized.

name: Owner Distribution Audit
description: Analyze owner workload and imbalance
prompt:
Get all {objectType} grouped by hubspot_owner_id. For each owner report:
- Number of assigned records
- Number with recent activity (last 30 days)
- Number with no activity (> 30 days)

Flag owners with > 200 assigned contacts or > 50 assigned deals as overloaded.
Flag records with no owner.

name: Lifecycle Accuracy Audit
description: Detect lifecycle stage mismatches
prompt:
Get all contacts grouped by lifecycle stage. Report:
- Count per stage
- Contacts in 'subscriber' with activity in last 30 days (should be upgraded)
- Contacts in 'lead' or 'MQL' with an associated closed-won deal (should be 'customer')
- Contacts with no lifecycle stage set

Provide recommendations for stage corrections.

name: Create Follow-Up Tasks
description: Bulk create tasks for matching records
prompt:
For each {objectType} that matches this criteria: {criteria}
Create a task assigned to the record owner:
- Subject: "{taskPrefix} {subject}"
- Body: "{body}"
- Due date: {dueDays} days from now
- Priority: {priority}

Before creating, show me the count of records that match and the list of owners who will receive tasks. Wait for my confirmation.

parameters.name: body
parameters.default: Please follow up with this record.

name: Fix Missing Associations
description: Auto-associate contacts to companies by name match
prompt:
Find contacts with a non-empty 'company' property but no company association.
For each, search companies by exact name match.
- If exactly 1 match: create the association. Log it.
- If 0 matches: log as 'no company found'.
- If 2+ matches: log as 'ambiguous — manual review needed'.

Show summary before executing. Wait for confirmation.

name: Update Lifecycle Stages
description: Bulk update lifecycle stages for matching contacts

parameters.name: criteria
parameters.default: lifecyclestage = subscriber AND last activity > 30 days

name: Lead Routing Workflow
description: Route new form submissions to sales
prompt:
Create a workflow:
Workflow: [VD] Lead Routing - {formName}
Object: contact
Trigger: Contact submits form {formId}
Actions:
  1. Set property lifecyclestage = lead
  2. Create task for default owner: "Route new lead from {formName}"
  3. Send internal email to sales manager: "New lead submitted {formName}"

Deploy disabled. Use portal config for owner IDs and form IDs.

name: Stalled Deal Alert Workflow
description: Alert owners when deals stall in a stage
prompt:
Create a workflow:
Workflow: [VD] Stalled Deal Alert
Object: deal
Trigger: Deal has been in same stage > {days} days
Actions:
  1. Send internal email to deal owner: "Deal {dealname} has been in {stage} for {days}+ days"
  2. Create task for deal owner: "Review stalled deal {dealname}" due in 2 days

Deploy disabled.

name: MQL Follow-Up Sequence
description: Automated follow-up when contact becomes MQL
prompt:
Create a workflow:
Workflow: [VD] MQL Follow-Up Sequence
Object: contact
Trigger: Lifecycle stage becomes MQL
Actions:
  1. Create task for owner: "Follow up with new MQL" due in 1 day, high priority
  2. Delay 3 days
  3. If no meeting booked (hs_meetings_booked = 0):
     Send internal email to sales manager: "MQL has not been contacted in 3 days"
  4. If meeting booked:
     Set property lead_status = "Connected"

Deploy disabled.

name: Customer Onboarding Workflow
description: Trigger onboarding tasks when deal closes
prompt:
Create a workflow:
Workflow: [VD] Customer Onboarding
Object: deal
Trigger: Deal stage becomes Closed Won
Actions:
  1. Set associated contact lifecycle stage = customer
  2. Create task for CS owner: "Schedule onboarding call for {dealname}" due in 1 day
  3. Delay 7 days
  4. Create task for CS owner: "Check onboarding progress for {dealname}"
  5. Send internal email to account manager: "Onboarding check for {dealname}"

Deploy disabled.

name: Name Standardization Script
description: Capitalize names and clean text fields
prompt:
Fetch all contacts. For each contact:
- Capitalize first letter of firstname and lastname
- Trim whitespace from all text properties
- Lowercase email addresses

Generate a script with dry-run mode. Show count of affected records before executing.

name: Deal Data Cleanup Script
description: Find and fix deal data issues
prompt:
Find all deals with:
- Amount = 0 or null → create task for owner: "Set deal amount for {dealname}"
- Close date in past + open stage → create task for owner: "Update close date or stage for {dealname}"
- No associated contact → log for manual review

Generate script. Run dry-run first.

name: Lifecycle Migration Script
description: Bulk update lifecycle stages based on activity
prompt:
Update lifecycle stages based on activity and deal status:
- Subscriber + form submission in 90 days → Lead
- Lead + meeting booked → MQL
- MQL + associated open deal → SQL
- Any stage + associated closed-won deal → Customer

Generate script. Dry-run first. Log all transitions.

name: Create Lead Scoring Properties
description: Set up lead scoring custom properties
prompt:
Create these contact properties in group "lead_scoring_custom":
1. lead_segment (enumeration, select): Enterprise, Mid-Market, SMB, Unknown
2. lead_source_detail (string, text): Free text for detailed source
3. lead_quality_score (number): 0-100 internal score
4. days_since_last_activity (number): Calculated field placeholder

Create the property group first if it doesn't exist.

name: Property Cleanup Audit
description: Find unused and duplicate properties for cleanup
prompt:
List all custom properties for contacts. Find properties that:
- Have < 5% fill rate (deletion candidates)
- Have names containing 'test', 'temp', 'old', 'deprecated'
- Are duplicates (similar names or labels)

Output a table: name, label, type, fill rate, recommendation (keep/review/delete).

name: Create Hot Leads List
description: Smart list of active MQLs above score threshold
prompt:
Create smart list "Hot Leads - Active MQLs":
- Lifecycle stage = MQL
- Lead score > {threshold}
- Last activity within 7 days
- Has associated company

name: Create At-Risk Customers List
description: Smart list of customers with no recent activity
prompt:
Create smart list "At Risk Customers":
- Lifecycle stage = Customer
- No activity in 60 days
- Has open ticket with priority High

name: Meeting Notes Analysis
description: Extract contacts, properties, pipelines, workflows, and lists from meeting notes into an implementation plan
prompt:
Paste meeting/discovery notes and extract:
- Contacts mentioned (name, email, role, company)
- Companies mentioned
- Custom properties needed (with type, fieldType, object)
- Pipelines needed (with stages, probabilities)
- Workflows needed (with triggers, actions)
- Lists needed (with filters, segmentation criteria)

Output a structured implementation plan with dependency order:
1. Property Groups
2. Properties
3. Pipelines
4. Lists
5. Workflows

For each item include:
- Name and description
- Technical spec summary
- Estimated effort (small/medium/large)
- Dependencies on other items

Present the plan and wait for confirmation before proceeding to create any resources.

name: Meeting Action Items to Tasks
description: Extract action items from meeting notes and create HubSpot tasks
prompt:
Extract action items from the meeting notes provided. For each action item:
- Identify the responsible person (owner)
- Determine due date based on urgency mentioned
- Set priority (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW)
- Write a clear task description

Separate action items into two groups:
1. HubSpot tasks (things that need to be done inside HubSpot)
2. External tasks (things outside HubSpot scope)

For HubSpot tasks, prepare to create them via the CRM API with:
- Subject line
- Task body with context from the meeting
- Owner assignment
- Due date
- Priority

Show the complete list of tasks with all details before creating. Wait for confirmation before creating any tasks.

name: CRM Setup Plan from Kickoff
description: Generate a complete CRM setup plan from client kickoff or discovery notes
prompt:
From client kickoff/discovery notes, generate a complete CRM setup plan:

1. Property Groups to create (per object type)
2. Custom Properties per object type (contacts, companies, deals, tickets) with:
   - Internal name, label, type, fieldType
   - Options for enumeration types
   - Which group they belong to
3. Deal Pipeline with stages:
   - Stage names, display order, probabilities
   - Closed Won and Closed Lost stages
4. Ticket Pipeline (if support process discussed):
   - Stage names for support workflow
5. Key Workflows for automation:
   - Trigger, actions, branching logic
   - Named with [VD] prefix
6. Smart Lists for segmentation:
   - Filter criteria, dynamic vs manual

Output as a numbered checklist with dependency order. Each item should have:
- [ ] checkbox format
- Resource type label
- Technical details
- Dependencies noted

Wait for confirmation before executing any step.

name: Create Deal Pipeline
description: Create a deal pipeline with stages, probabilities, and required Closed Won/Lost stages
prompt:
Create a deal pipeline named "{pipelineName}" with these stages: {stages}

For each stage, configure:
- label: Stage name
- displayOrder: Sequential starting from 0
- probability: Between 0 and 1, increasing as stages progress

MUST include these closing stages at the end:
- Closed Won: probability 1.0, metadata: { "isClosed": "true", "closedWon": "true" }
- Closed Lost: probability 0, metadata: { "isClosed": "true", "closedWon": "false" }

Validation checks before deploying:
- Probabilities should generally increase across stages
- No duplicate stage names
- Closed Won and Closed Lost are present

Deploy via create_pipeline MCP tool. Report the created pipeline ID and stage IDs.

parameters.name: stages
parameters.default: Discovery, Qualification, Proposal, Negotiation, Closed Won, Closed Lost

name: Create Ticket Pipeline
description: Create a support ticket pipeline with standard stages
prompt:
Create a ticket pipeline named "{pipelineName}" with these stages: {stages}

For each stage, configure:
- label: Stage name
- displayOrder: Sequential starting from 0

The final stage MUST have metadata: { "isClosed": "true" }
All other stages should NOT have isClosed metadata.

Typical ticket pipeline stages if none specified:
- New (displayOrder: 0)
- Waiting on Contact (displayOrder: 1)
- Waiting on Us (displayOrder: 2)
- In Progress (displayOrder: 3)
- Closed (displayOrder: 4, isClosed: true)

Deploy via create_pipeline MCP tool for objectType "tickets".

parameters.name: stages
parameters.default: New, Waiting on Contact, Waiting on Us, In Progress, Closed

name: Pipeline Audit & Fix
description: Audit all pipelines for missing stages, configuration issues, and recommend fixes
prompt:
List all deal and ticket pipelines using list_pipelines for both objectTypes.

For each pipeline, check:
- Missing Closed Won stage (deals only) — CRITICAL
- Missing Closed Lost stage (deals only) — CRITICAL
- Stages with 0% probability that are NOT Closed Lost — WARNING
- Duplicate stage names within the same pipeline — WARNING
- Stages with probability not increasing in display order — INFO
- Empty pipelines with no associated records — INFO

Run audit_pipelines for both deals and tickets to get additional insights.

Report findings grouped by severity (CRITICAL, WARNING, INFO).
For each finding, provide:
- Pipeline name and ID
- Issue description
- Recommended fix
- Whether the fix can be automated

name: Reorder Pipeline Stages
description: View and reorder pipeline stages with validation
prompt:
List current stages for {objectType} pipeline {pipelineId}.

Show current order as a numbered list with:
- Stage name
- Current displayOrder
- Probability (deals only)
- isClosed status

Accept a new order from the user (e.g., "3, 1, 2, 4, 5" to reorder).

Validation before applying:
- Closed stages (isClosed=true) must remain at the end
- Probabilities should still make logical sense in new order
- No stages can be removed during reorder

Update displayOrder values via the pipeline API. Show the updated order for confirmation.

name: Search Workflows
description: Search all workflows by name pattern and show key details
prompt:
Search all workflows matching the name pattern "{pattern}".

List all workflows using list_workflows. Filter results where the workflow name contains "{pattern}" (case-insensitive).

For each match, show:
- Name
- Flow ID
- Type (CONTACT_FLOW / PLATFORM_FLOW)
- Enabled/Disabled status
- Object type (contacts, deals, companies, tickets)
- Brief enrollment criteria summary

Sort by name. Show total count of matches.

This is useful for finding existing workflows before creating duplicates or for locating workflows to clone/modify.

name: Clone Workflow
description: Clone an existing workflow under a new name with [VD] prefix
prompt:
Clone workflow with flow ID {flowId} as "[VD] {newName}".

Steps:
1. Get the full spec of the source workflow using get_workflow
2. Show the source workflow summary: name, type, enrollment criteria, number of actions
3. Change the name to "[VD] {newName}"
4. Deploy as a new workflow (always disabled)
5. Report the new flow ID

If the source workflow uses any broken action types (0-9 in-app notification, 0-11 rotate owner), flag them and suggest replacements before deploying.

name: Enable/Disable Workflows
description: List workflow states and toggle enable/disable for specific workflows
prompt:
List all workflows using list_workflows. Show a table with:
- Flow ID
- Name
- Enabled/Disabled status
- Type

Filter to show only workflows matching the action: if {action} is "enable", show disabled workflows. If {action} is "disable", show enabled workflows.

For workflow {flowId}:
- If enabling: WARN that enabling a workflow immediately starts enrolling matching records. Confirm before proceeding.
- If disabling: Proceed with confirmation.

Note: The MCP tools may not support direct enable/disable. If not available, provide the manual steps to toggle in HubSpot UI.

name: Workflow Actions Audit
description: Analyze a workflow's actions for broken types, invalid references, and issues
prompt:
Get the full spec of workflow {flowId} using get_workflow.

Analyze the workflow and report:

1. Action Types Used:
   - List each action with: actionId, type, actionTypeId, brief description
   - Flag any using broken types:
     * 0-9 (In-app notification) — BROKEN, replace with 0-8 (Internal email)
     * 0-11 (Rotate owner) — BROKEN, replace with 0-5 (Set property on hubspot_owner_id)

2. Enrollment Criteria:
   - Show the enrollment trigger
   - Validate filter structure (OR → AND → filters hierarchy)
   - Check operationType matches property type

3. Property References:
   - List all properties referenced in enrollment criteria and actions
   - Verify each property exists using list_properties
   - Flag missing properties

4. Branching Logic:
   - Map the action flow: start → branches → end
   - Check for dead-end actions (no connection to next action)
   - Verify IF_BRANCH has both acceptActions and rejectActions

Provide fix recommendations for each issue found.

name: Workflow Dependency Map
description: Map all workflow dependencies on properties, pipelines, and lists
prompt:
List all workflows using list_workflows. For each workflow, get the full spec using get_workflow.

Extract and catalog:
1. Properties referenced in enrollment criteria (filter conditions)
2. Properties set by SET_PROPERTY actions
3. Properties read in IF_BRANCH conditions
4. Pipelines/stages referenced in enrollment or actions
5. Lists referenced in enrollment criteria
6. Other workflows referenced (if any chaining)

Output a dependency matrix:

**Properties → Workflows that READ them:**
(property name → list of workflow names)

**Properties → Workflows that WRITE them:**
(property name → list of workflow names)

**Pipelines → Workflows:**
(pipeline/stage → list of workflow names)

**Lists → Workflows:**
(list name → list of workflow names)

Highlight:
- Properties that are written by one workflow and read by another (chains)
- Properties referenced by workflows but that don't exist in the portal
- Circular dependencies between workflows

name: Create Template Bundle
description: Bundle properties, pipelines, workflows, and lists into a reusable template
prompt:
Create a reusable template bundle named "{templateName}".

Description: {description}

Steps:
1. Ask which resources to include:
   - Properties (list available custom properties)
   - Pipelines (list available pipelines)
   - Workflows (list available workflows)
   - Lists (list available lists)

2. For each selected resource, fetch the full spec.

3. Validate dependencies:
   - If a workflow references a property, that property MUST be in the template
   - If a workflow references a pipeline stage, that pipeline MUST be in the template
   - If a workflow references a list, that list MUST be in the template
   - Flag any missing dependencies

4. Save as a template draft using save_template_draft with:
   - name
   - description
   - version tag
   - all resource specs bundled

Report the template ID and contents summary.

name: Dry-Run Template Install
description: Preview what a template install would create without making changes
prompt:
Run a dry-run install of template {templateId} on the active portal.

Use install_template with dryRun: true.

Report:
1. Resources that WOULD be created:
   - Properties (name, type, object)
   - Pipelines (name, stages count)
   - Workflows (name, type)
   - Lists (name, type)

2. Resources that already EXIST (would be skipped):
   - Show matching existing resource name and ID

3. Potential CONFLICTS:
   - Properties with same internal name but different type
   - Pipelines with same name
   - Workflows with same name

Do NOT create anything. This is a preview only.

Ask if the user wants to proceed with the actual install after reviewing.

name: Export Portal Config
description: Export the current portal's full configuration as a reusable template
prompt:
Export the current portal's complete configuration as a reusable template named "{templateName}".

Use export_portal_template to capture:
- All custom property groups
- All custom properties (contacts, companies, deals, tickets)
- All deal pipelines with stages
- All ticket pipelines with stages
- All automation workflows (names, enrollment criteria, actions)
- All active lists (dynamic and manual)

Save as a template draft.

Report summary:
- Total properties exported (by object type)
- Total pipelines exported (deals + tickets)
- Total workflows exported
- Total lists exported
- Template ID for future reference

Note: Owner IDs and form IDs in workflows will need to be reconfigured when installing on a different portal.

name: Compare Template Versions
description: Compare two versions of a template to see added, removed, and modified resources
prompt:
Compare template {templateId} version {v1} vs version {v2}.

For each resource type (properties, pipelines, workflows, lists), show:

**Added in v{v2}:**
- Resources present in v{v2} but not in v{v1}

**Removed in v{v2}:**
- Resources present in v{v1} but not in v{v2}

**Modified:**
- Resources present in both but with differences
- Show before (v{v1}) and after (v{v2}) for each change

**Breaking Changes:**
- Properties with type changes (cannot be changed after creation)
- Removed properties that are referenced by workflows in v{v2}
- Pipeline stage removals that may affect existing deals

Provide a migration recommendation: safe to upgrade, needs review, or breaking changes require manual intervention.

name: Search CRM Records
description: Search CRM records with filters and display results in a table

name: Create CRM Record
description: Create a single CRM record with validation of required fields
prompt:
Create a single {objectType} record with these properties: {properties}

Validate required fields before creating:
- Contacts: must have email
- Companies: must have name
- Deals: must have dealname, pipeline, dealstage
- Tickets: must have subject

If required fields are missing, report which ones and ask for values.

Use create_record MCP tool. Show the created record ID and a summary of the record.

If the record references a pipeline or stage, validate they exist first.

name: Batch Update Records
description: Find records matching a filter and batch update a property value
prompt:
Find all {objectType} matching: {filterProperty} {operator} {filterValue}

Update property {updateProperty} to "{newValue}" for all matching records.

Before executing:
1. Search for matching records using search_records
2. Show the total count of matching records
3. Show a sample of 5 records with current value of {updateProperty}
4. Wait for explicit confirmation

After confirmation, use batch_upsert_records for efficient bulk updates (up to 100 per batch).

Report:
- Total records updated
- Any errors encountered
- Sample of 3 updated records to verify

name: Merge Duplicate Records
description: Find and merge duplicate records by matching property
prompt:
Find potential duplicate {objectType} by matching on {matchProperty}.

Steps:
1. Search all {objectType} and group by {matchProperty} value
2. Identify groups with 2+ records sharing the same value
3. For each group of duplicates, show:
   - All record IDs
   - Key properties for comparison (name, email, createdate, last activity)
   - Recommended primary record (most complete data, most recent activity)
4. Present the merge plan: which records merge into which primary
5. Wait for explicit confirmation

Note: Record merging may not be available via MCP tools. If not, provide the list of duplicate groups with merge recommendations for manual action in HubSpot UI.

name: GDPR Delete Contact
description: Permanently delete a contact and all associated data per GDPR requirements
prompt:
GDPR permanent deletion request for contact {contactId}.

This is IRREVERSIBLE. Follow this strict process:

1. Fetch the contact record using get_record to show full details:
   - Email address
   - Name
   - Associated companies, deals, tickets
   - Creation date
   - Total records that will be affected

2. Display all details and warn:
   "This will PERMANENTLY delete this contact and all associated data. This cannot be undone."

3. Ask the user to confirm by typing back the exact email address of the contact.

4. Verify the typed email matches the contact record exactly.

5. Only then proceed with the GDPR deletion.

Note: GDPR deletion may not be available via MCP tools. If not available, provide the manual steps to execute in HubSpot UI under Privacy & Consent > GDPR Delete.

name: Export Full Portal Config
description: Export complete portal configuration as a structured JSON template for re-import
prompt:
Export the complete portal configuration as a structured template.

Gather all resources:
1. Custom properties for all object types (contacts, companies, deals, tickets):
   - Use list_properties for each object type
   - Filter to custom properties only (exclude HubSpot defaults)
   - Include: name, label, type, fieldType, groupName, options

2. Property groups:
   - Extract unique group names from properties

3. Deal pipelines with stages:
   - Use list_pipelines for deals
   - Include stage labels, order, probabilities, metadata

4. Ticket pipelines with stages:
   - Use list_pipelines for tickets

5. Workflows:
   - Use list_workflows
   - For each, include: name, type, enrollment criteria summary, action count

6. Active lists:
   - Use list_lists
   - Include: name, type (DYNAMIC/MANUAL), filter summary

Format as a structured JSON template compatible with install_template.
Save as a template draft using save_template_draft.

Report total counts for each resource type.

name: Clone Workflow to Template
description: Export a workflow as a portable template, stripping portal-specific IDs
prompt:
Get the full spec of workflow {flowId} using get_workflow.

Create a portable workflow template by:
1. Stripping portal-specific IDs:
   - Owner IDs → replace with placeholder {owner_id}
   - Form IDs → replace with placeholder {form_id}
   - List IDs → replace with placeholder {list_id}
   - Team IDs → replace with placeholder {team_id}

2. Preserving the workflow structure:
   - Enrollment criteria
   - All actions and branching
   - Property references (these are portable)
   - Delay configurations

3. Adding configuration notes:
   - List all placeholders that need to be filled when deploying to a new portal
   - Note any properties that must exist on the target portal
   - Note any pipelines/stages referenced

4. Save as a workflow draft using save_workflow_draft

Report:
- Template workflow name
- Number of actions preserved
- Number of placeholders to configure
- List of required properties on target portal

name: Export Property Audit Report
description: Export a property audit as a formatted table with fill rates and recommendations
prompt:
List all custom properties for {objectType} using list_properties.

For each property, collect:
- Internal name
- Display label
- Type (string, number, enumeration, etc.)
- Field type (text, select, checkbox, etc.)
- Group name
- Fill rate estimate (use audit_properties if available)

Output as a formatted table suitable for CSV export:
name | label | type | fieldType | groupName | fillRate | recommendation

Recommendations:
- Fill rate < 5%: "DELETE CANDIDATE - Very low usage"
- Fill rate 5-20%: "REVIEW - Low usage, consider consolidating"
- Fill rate 20-80%: "KEEP - Moderate usage"
- Fill rate > 80%: "KEEP - High usage"
- Names containing 'test', 'temp', 'old': "REVIEW - Possibly temporary"

Summary stats:
- Total custom properties
- Delete candidates count
- Review candidates count
- Properties by group

name: Safe Workflow Deploy
description: Deploy a workflow with comprehensive safety checks and validation
prompt:
Deploy a workflow with all safety checks.

Pre-deployment validation:
1. Run deep_health_check on the active portal to verify connectivity and action type support
2. Parse the workflow spec and validate structure:
   - name starts with [VD]
   - isEnabled is false
   - nextAvailableActionId is a string
   - All actionIds are strings
3. Check all referenced properties exist using list_properties
4. Check all action types are supported on this portal:
   - Flag 0-9 (in-app notification) — suggest 0-8 (internal email) instead
   - Flag 0-11 (rotate owner) — suggest 0-5 (set hubspot_owner_id) instead
5. Validate enrollment criteria structure (OR → AND → filters)
6. Validate operationType matches property types

If all checks pass:
- Deploy using deploy_workflow with allowPartial: true
- Report the flowId
- Report any partial-install manual steps

If checks fail:
- Report all issues
- Suggest fixes
- Do NOT deploy until issues are resolved

name: Install Template with Checks
description: Install a template with dry-run preview and confirmation
prompt:
Install template {templateId} with safety checks.

Steps:
1. Run install_template with dryRun: true first
2. Show what will be created:
   - Properties (count and names)
   - Pipelines (count and names)
   - Workflows (count and names)
   - Lists (count and names)
3. Show what already exists (will be skipped)
4. Show any conflicts or warnings
5. Wait for explicit user confirmation

6. After confirmation, run install_template with dryRun: false
7. Report results:
   - Successfully created resources
   - Skipped resources
   - Any partial-install results for workflows
   - Manual steps needed (from manualSteps array)

ALWAYS surface manualSteps to the user — these are workflow actions that could not be auto-deployed and need manual configuration in HubSpot UI.

name: Execute Config Bundle
description: Execute a configuration bundle with dependency ordering and safety checks
prompt:
Execute a configuration bundle with full dependency ordering.

Steps:
1. Validate all resources in the config using validate_config
2. Run execute_config with dryRun: true to check for conflicts
3. Show the execution plan in dependency order:
   a. Property Groups (created first)
   b. Properties (depend on groups)
   c. Pipelines (independent)
   d. Lists (may depend on properties)
   e. Workflows (depend on properties, pipelines, lists)
4. Show any conflicts or existing resources
5. Wait for explicit user confirmation

6. After confirmation, run execute_config with dryRun: false
7. Resources are created in dependency order automatically
8. Report results:
   - Each resource: created / skipped / failed
   - Any partial-install results for workflows
   - Manual steps needed
   - Total time elapsed

If any resource fails, report the error and continue with remaining resources where possible.

name: Portal Health Check
description: Run a comprehensive health check on the active portal
prompt:
Run a comprehensive health check on the active portal.

1. Test API connectivity:
   - Call list_portals to verify connection
   - Call get_active_portal to confirm active portal

2. Validate OAuth scopes:
   - Call portal_capabilities to get granted scopes
   - Check for required scopes:
     * crm.objects.contacts (read/write)
     * crm.objects.companies (read/write)
     * crm.objects.deals (read/write)
     * crm.schemas (read/write)
     * automation (read/write)
   - Flag missing scopes

3. Check workflow action types:
   - Call deep_health_check to test which action types work
   - Report status for: 0-1 (Delay), 0-3 (Create task), 0-5 (Set property), 0-8 (Internal email), 0-35 (Delay alternate)
   - Flag broken types: 0-9 (In-app notification), 0-11 (Rotate owner)

4. Check for deprecation warnings

Report:
- Connectivity: OK / FAILED
- Scopes: X of Y required scopes granted
- Action Types: X of Y working
- Warnings: list any issues
- Overall Status: HEALTHY / DEGRADED / CRITICAL

name: Portal Scope Audit
description: Audit OAuth scopes and flag missing permissions
prompt:
List all granted OAuth scopes on the active portal using portal_capabilities.

Compare against the full list of required scopes for VeroDigital operations:

Required for CRM:
- crm.objects.contacts.read / .write
- crm.objects.companies.read / .write
- crm.objects.deals.read / .write
- crm.objects.owners.read
- crm.schemas.contacts.read / .write
- crm.schemas.companies.read / .write
- crm.schemas.deals.read / .write

Required for Automation:
- automation.read / .write

Required for Lists:
- crm.lists.read / .write

Optional but recommended:
- crm.objects.custom.read / .write
- crm.objects.marketing_events.read
- files.read / .write

Report:
- Granted scopes (grouped by category)
- Missing required scopes (CRITICAL)
- Missing optional scopes (INFO)
- Provide guidance on how to add missing scopes via the OAuth reauthorization flow

name: New Portal Setup Guide
description: Step-by-step guided setup for a new portal with VeroDigital defaults
prompt:
Step-by-step guide to set up a new portal with VeroDigital defaults.

Execute each step in order, waiting for confirmation between major steps:

Step 1 - Verify Connection:
- Run list_portals and confirm the target portal
- Run deep_health_check to verify capabilities
- Report any scope limitations

Step 2 - Create Standard Property Groups:
- lead_scoring_custom (Contact)
- sales_custom (Deal)
- cs_custom (Ticket)
- company_custom (Company)

Step 3 - Create Core Custom Properties:
- Contact: lead_segment, lead_source_detail, lead_quality_score
- Deal: deal_type, competitor, loss_reason
- Company: company_tier, account_manager

Step 4 - Set Up Deal Pipeline:
- Discovery → Qualification → Proposal → Negotiation → Closed Won → Closed Lost

Step 5 - Create Key Workflows:
- [VD] Lead Routing
- [VD] Stalled Deal Alert
- [VD] MQL Follow-Up
- [VD] Customer Onboarding

Step 6 - Create Smart Lists:
- Hot Leads (MQL + recent activity)
- At Risk Customers (no activity 60 days)

Report progress after each step. Total estimated time: 15-20 minutes.

name: Portal Capabilities Report
description: Generate a full capabilities report for the active portal
prompt:
Generate a complete capabilities report for the active portal.

Gather data from multiple sources:

1. Portal Identity:
   - Call get_active_portal for: portal name, hub ID, environment
   - Call list_portals for connection status

2. OAuth Scopes:
   - Call portal_capabilities for all granted scopes
   - Categorize by: CRM, Automation, Lists, Files, Custom Objects

3. Action Type Support:
   - Call deep_health_check for supported workflow action types
   - List working vs broken action types

4. Current Resource Counts:
   - Custom properties count per object type (list_properties)
   - Pipeline count for deals and tickets (list_pipelines)
   - Workflow count (list_workflows)
   - List count (list_lists)

5. Portal Tier Assessment:
   - Based on available scopes, estimate: Free, Starter, Professional, Enterprise
   - Flag tier-limited features

Output as a structured report with sections for each category.
Include a summary section with key limitations and recommendations.

name: Audit Portal to ClickUp Tasks
description: Run a portal audit and create ClickUp tasks for each finding
prompt:
Run a comprehensive portal audit and create ClickUp tasks for each finding.

Step 1 - Run Audits:
- Property audit: audit_properties for contacts, companies, deals
- Pipeline audit: audit_pipelines for deals and tickets
- Data quality checks: missing required fields, orphaned records

Step 2 - Categorize Findings:
Group each finding by category:
- Data Quality (missing fields, bad data)
- Properties (unused, duplicates, low fill rate)
- Pipelines (missing stages, configuration issues)
- Workflows (broken actions, missing references)

Step 3 - Create ClickUp Tasks:
For each finding, create a ClickUp task in folder {clickupFolderId} with:
- Title: "[Category] Brief description"
- Description: Full technical details, current state, recommended fix
- Priority: urgent (data loss risk), high (broken functionality), normal (improvement), low (cleanup)
- Assignee: {assignee}
- Due date: urgent = 2 days, high = 5 days, normal = 10 days, low = 30 days

Show the full list of tasks before creating. Wait for confirmation.

name: Implementation Plan to ClickUp Board
description: Convert an implementation plan into ClickUp tasks with dependencies
prompt:
From the implementation plan provided, extract each action item and create ClickUp tasks.

For each action item, create a ClickUp task in folder {clickupFolderId} with:
- Title: Clear, actionable task name
- Description: Full technical details including:
  * What needs to be done
  * Technical specifications (property types, workflow logic, etc.)
  * Acceptance criteria
- Dependencies: Link to blocking tasks (tasks that must complete first)
- Priority: Based on dependency chain position (foundational = high, dependent = normal)
- Estimated effort: small (< 30 min), medium (30-60 min), large (> 60 min)
- Due date: Based on dependency chain and effort

Create tasks in dependency order so blocking tasks exist before their dependents:
1. Property groups and properties first
2. Pipelines second
3. Lists third
4. Workflows last (they depend on all above)

After creating all tasks, add dependency links between them using clickup_add_task_dependency.

Show a summary board view: task name | status | priority | depends on | due date
